title: Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted

seo.title: Authentic Lamb Ouzi Recipe: Whole Slow Roasted Perfection
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Middle Eastern Lamb Ouzi! Our slow-roasted whole lamb recipe is perfect for special occasions. Get the full recipe here.
lowCompetitionKeywords: slow cooked lamb ouzi with rice
seo.h1: Authentic Lamb Ouzi: Whole Slow Roasted Middle Eastern Recipe
h2Headings: Ingredients You'll Need for Lamb Ouzi
Step-by-Step Instructions for Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi
Pro Tips for the Best Lamb Ouzi Results
Variations and Substitutions for Lamb Ouzi
seo.imageAlt: A beautifully presented whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi dish with rice, nuts, and garnishes.
seo.imageCaption: Impress your guests with this show-stopping authentic Middle Eastern Lamb Ouzi, slow-roasted to perfection.

description: Lamb Ouzi is a majestic Middle Eastern dish, featuring a whole slow-roasted lamb shoulder or leg, tenderized to perfection and infused with aromatic spices. It's traditionally served over a bed of fragrant basmati rice, often adorned with toasted nuts and fresh herbs, making it the ultimate centerpiece for grand celebrations. The meat falls off the bone, melting in your mouth with every savory bite, a true testament to slow cooking mastery and a feast for all senses.

ingredients.item: Whole lamb shoulder or leg, bone-in

ingredients.notes: allspice, cardamom, cinnamon, black pepper, nutmeg

instructions.title: Prepare the Lamb Marinade
instructions.text: In a bowl, combine the Ouzi spice blend, salt, black pepper, minced garlic, and 2 tablespoons of olive oil or ghee. Score the lamb all over with a sharp knife and rub the spice mixture generously into the lamb, ensuring it penetrates the scores. For best results, let it marinate in the refrigerator overnight, or for at least 4 hours.

instructions.title: Sear the Lamb
instructions.text: Preheat a large, heavy-bottomed roasting pan or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add a little more olive oil or ghee if needed. Carefully sear the marinated lamb on all sides until deeply browned, about 5-7 minutes per side. This crucial step builds incredible flavor and a beautiful crust.

instructions.title: Slow Roast the Lamb
instructions.text: Add the chopped onions around the lamb in the roasting pan. Pour in 2 cups of lamb broth or water. Cover the pan tightly with a lid or heavy-duty foil. Transfer to a preheated oven at 150°C (300°F). Slow roast for 4-5 hours, or until the lamb is fork-tender and easily pulls away from the bone. Baste occasionally with the pan juices to keep it moist.

instructions.title: Prepare the Fragrant Rice
instructions.text: About 45 minutes before the lamb is done, drain the soaked basmati rice. In a separate large pot, heat 1 tablespoon olive oil or ghee. Sauté 1 chopped onion until translucent. Add the drained rice, 1 teaspoon of Ouzi spice blend, and 4 cups of lamb broth (or water). Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes until the liquid is absorbed and the rice is fluffy.

instructions.title: Rest the Lamb and Finish Rice
instructions.text: Once the lamb is tender, remove it from the oven and let it rest, covered, for at least 20-30 minutes before carving. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring a succulent result. Fluff the cooked basmati rice with a fork. You can also mix some of the flavorful lamb pan juices into the rice for extra depth.

instructions.title: Assemble and Serve
instructions.text: On a large serving platter, spread the fragrant basmati rice evenly. Carefully place the whole slow-roasted lamb on top of the rice. Garnish generously with toasted pine nuts, slivered almonds, and fresh chopped parsley or cilantro. Serve immediately as a grand centerpiece for your meal, perhaps with a side of plain yogurt or a fresh Middle Eastern salad.

tips: For the most tender lamb, ensure your roasting pan is tightly sealed. If using foil, create a double layer and crimp it securely around the edges to trap all the steam and moisture, which is key for slow roasting.

The Ouzi spice blend is crucial for authentic flavor. If you cant find a pre-made blend, mix equal parts ground allspice, cardamom, cinnamon, black pepper, and a pinch of nutmeg for a fragrant, homemade alternative.

Toasted nuts add a delightful crunch and richness. Toast them gently in a dry pan or oven until fragrant and golden, watching closely to prevent burning, then sprinkle just before serving to maintain their crispness and aroma.
article:
Embark on a culinary journey to the heart of the Middle East with a dish that epitomizes celebration and hospitality: Lamb Ouzi. This magnificent meal, often reserved for special occasions and family gatherings, showcases the tender succulence of slow-roasted lamb paired with aromatic, spiced rice and a delightful mix of nuts and dried fruits. While the idea of roasting a whole lamb cut might seem daunting, the process is surprisingly straightforward, and the rewards are immeasurable. Prepare to impress your guests and delight your senses with this truly unforgettable Middle Eastern dinner recipe.

## What is Lamb Ouzi?

Lamb Ouzi, sometimes spelled "Ozzy" or "Ouzy," is a cornerstone of Middle Eastern cuisine, particularly cherished in countries like Jordan, Syria, Lebanon, and Palestine. At its heart, it's a grand platter featuring exceptionally tender, often shredded, lamb served atop a bed of richly spiced rice. The term "Ouzi" itself can refer to the entire dish, or sometimes specifically to the spiced rice mixture that accompanies the lamb. Unlike a simple roast, Lamb Ouzi is a symphony of flavors and textures, combining the savory depth of lamb with the sweetness of dried fruits, the crunch of toasted nuts, and the earthy warmth of traditional Middle Eastern spices like allspice, cinnamon, and cardamom. It’s more than just a meal; it’s a centerpiece, a symbol of generosity, and a dish designed to be shared.

## Ingredients You'll Need for Lamb Ouzi

To create an authentic and show-stopping Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi, you'll need quality ingredients. While exact quantities can vary based on the size of your lamb cut and number of servings, here’s a comprehensive list:

For the Lamb:
*   1 bone-in lamb shoulder or leg (4-6 lbs, or larger if feeding a crowd)
*   4-5 cloves garlic, minced
*   2 tablespoons olive oil
*   1 tablespoon ground allspice
*   1 teaspoon ground cinnamon
*   1 teaspoon ground cardamom
*   1/2 teaspoon ground nutmeg
*   Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
*   2-3 bay leaves
*   1 large onion, quartered
*   4-6 cups lamb or chicken broth (for braising)

For the Spiced Rice:
*   3 cups basmati rice, rinsed until water runs clear
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   2 tablespoons olive oil or ghee
*   1 teaspoon ground allspice
*   1/2 teaspoon ground cinnamon
*   1/4 teaspoon ground cardamom
*   1/2 cup pine nuts, lightly toasted
*   1/2 cup slivered almonds, lightly toasted
*   1/2 cup raisins or dried cranberries (optional)
*   4.5-5 cups lamb or chicken broth
*   Salt to taste

For Garnish (Optional):
*   Fresh parsley, chopped
*   Additional toasted nuts (pine nuts, almonds)

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi

Preparing this impressive Lamb Ouzi requires patience, but the steps are straightforward and rewarding.

1.  **Prepare the Lamb:** Pat the lamb shoulder or leg dry with paper towels. In a small bowl, combine minced garlic, olive oil, allspice, cinnamon, cardamom, nutmeg, salt, and pepper to create a rub. Generously massage this spice mixture all over the lamb, ensuring it gets into all crevices. For best results, cover and refrigerate the lamb overnight to allow the flavors to penetrate deeply. If time is short, marinate for at least 2-3 hours at room temperature.
2.  **Slow Roast the Lamb:** Preheat your oven to 300°F (150°C). Place the marinated lamb in a large roasting pan. Add the quartered onion and bay leaves to the pan. Pour 4-6 cups of broth around the lamb (the liquid should come about 1-2 inches up the side of the pan, but not submerge the lamb). Cover the roasting pan tightly with foil. Roast for 3.5 to 5 hours, or until the lamb is fall-off-the-bone tender. The exact time will depend on the size of your lamb cut. Midway through, you can flip the lamb for even cooking.
3.  **Brown the Lamb:** Once tender, remove the foil. Increase the oven temperature to 400°F (200°C) and roast for another 20-30 minutes, or until the lamb skin is beautifully browned and slightly crispy. Baste occasionally with the pan juices.
4.  **Rest the Lamb:** Carefully remove the lamb from the oven and transfer it to a large cutting board or serving platter. Tent it loosely with foil and let it rest for at least 20-30 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring maximum tenderness.
5.  **Prepare the Spiced Rice:** While the lamb is resting, prepare the rice. In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat olive oil or ghee over medium heat. Add the finely chopped onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in the allspice, cinnamon, and cardamom and cook for another minute until fragrant. Add the rinsed basmati rice, stirring to coat the grains with the spices and oil. Pour in the broth and add salt to taste. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover tightly, and simmer for 15-18 minutes, or until all the liquid is absorbed and the rice is tender.
6.  **Finish the Rice:** Remove the rice from the heat and let it sit, covered, for 5 minutes. Gently fluff the rice with a fork. Stir in the toasted pine nuts, slivered almonds, and optional raisins/dried cranberries.
7.  **Assemble and Serve:** Shred the tender Lamb Ouzi meat directly onto a large serving platter, discarding the bone. Spoon the fragrant spiced rice around and under the lamb. Garnish generously with fresh chopped parsley and extra toasted nuts.

## Pro Tips for the Best Lamb Ouzi Results

Achieving a truly spectacular Lamb Ouzi involves a few key techniques:

*   **Marination is Key:** Don't skimp on marinating time. The longer the lamb sits in the spice rub, the more flavorful and tender it will become. Overnight is ideal.
*   **Low and Slow:** The secret to fall-off-the-bone lamb is slow roasting at a low temperature. This breaks down the connective tissues, resulting in incredibly tender meat.
*   **Bone-In for Flavor:** Opt for a bone-in lamb shoulder or leg. The bone adds significant flavor and helps the meat cook more evenly.
*   **Toast Your Nuts:** Lightly toasting the pine nuts and almonds before adding them to the rice dramatically enhances their flavor and aroma. Be careful not to burn them.
*   **Rest the Meat:** Always rest the lamb after roasting. This crucial step allows the meat fibers to relax and reabsorb juices, making it incredibly moist and tender.
*   **Quality Basmati:** Use good quality basmati rice. Its long grains and distinct aroma are perfect for absorbing the rich flavors of the Ouzi spices. Rinse it thoroughly to remove excess starch, preventing sticky rice.

## Serving Suggestions & Pairings

Lamb Ouzi is a feast in itself, but it shines even brighter when accompanied by complementary sides.

*   **Yogurt Sauce:** A simple, refreshing yogurt sauce (like plain yogurt mixed with minced garlic and dried mint, or a cucumber raita) provides a tangy counterpoint to the rich lamb and rice.
*   **Fresh Salads:** Crisp, fresh salads such as Fattoush (a bread salad with sumac) or Tabbouleh (parsley and bulgur salad) offer brightness and acidity, cutting through the richness.
*   **Pickles:** Assorted Middle Eastern pickles (like pickled turnips, cucumbers, or carrots) are excellent for cleansing the palate.
*   **Warm Pita Bread:** Serve with warm pita bread for scooping up every last bit of deliciousness.
*   **Beverages:** Traditional choices include Ayran (a savory yogurt drink) or a simple, refreshing mint tea.

## Variations and Substitutions for Lamb Ouzi

While the classic whole slow-roasted Lamb Ouzi is magnificent, you can adapt the recipe to suit different preferences or available ingredients.

*   **Lamb Cut:** If a whole shoulder or leg is too much, you can use lamb shanks or even smaller boneless lamb pieces (adjust cooking time accordingly). For a quicker version, use cubed lamb stew meat, but you'll lose some of the grandeur.
*   **Stuffing the Lamb:** For a truly elaborate Ouzi, some recipes involve deboning a whole lamb shoulder and stuffing the cavity with a portion of the spiced rice before rolling and roasting.
*   **Rice Variations:** You can add vermicelli noodles to the rice for added texture. Some recipes also include ground meat (beef or lamb) cooked into the rice.
*   **Nut and Dried Fruit Mix:** Feel free to experiment with different nuts like pistachios or walnuts, and other dried fruits such as dried apricots or dates.
*   **Vegetarian Ouzi:** While not traditional, a vegetarian version can be made by focusing on the spiced rice with a generous addition of roasted vegetables (eggplant, zucchini, carrots) and chickpeas, served as a main dish.

## Storage and Reheating Tips

Lamb Ouzi, especially the rice, can be quite substantial, so it's good to know how to store and reheat leftovers.

*   **Storage:** Store leftover lamb and rice separately in airtight containers in the refrigerator. The lamb will keep for 3-4 days, and the rice for 2-3 days.
*   **Freezing:** Lamb meat can be frozen for up to 2-3 months. The spiced rice can also be frozen, though its texture might be slightly softer upon thawing.
*   **Reheating Lamb:** For best results, reheat the lamb gently in the oven. Place it in an oven-safe dish with a splash of broth or water, cover with foil, and warm at 300°F (150°C) until heated through. Alternatively, microwave in short bursts.
*   **Reheating Rice:** Reheat the rice on the stovetop with a tablespoon or two of water or broth, covered, over low heat until warmed through, fluffing occasionally. You can also microwave it, adding a little moisture to prevent dryness.

Preparing Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi is a culinary endeavor that promises an extraordinary dining experience. The aroma filling your home, the tender lamb melting in your mouth, and the rich, complex flavors of the spiced rice will transport you straight to a Middle Eastern feast. Don't be intimidated by its grandeur; with a little time and love, you can create a masterpiece that will be remembered long after the last bite.
